title: hoya fauziana subsp. fauziana
intro: Hoya fauziana subsp. fauziana is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the waxflower or hoya. It is a climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits such as antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The herbal preparations of Hoya fauziana subsp. fauziana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
